1.title: From Adam to Abraham
1.text: The chronicler begins all human history with a single name: Adam. The genealogy descends from Adam through Noah to Abraham, placing Israel within God's plan for all mankind.

1.question: Which three sons of Noah are listed at the start of the chapter?
From which son of Ham do Seba, Havilah and the rest listed here descend?
The Arvadites, Zemarites and Hamathites descend from which son of Noah?
Ophir, Havilah and Jobab are listed as sons of whom?
What other name does the verse give to Abram?
Which two sons of Isaac are mentioned?
The sister of Lotan, named in this line of Edom, was called:
The kings listed (Bela, Jobab and others) reigned in which land?
After the death of Saul (of Rehoboth), who reigned in his place?
Magdiel and Iram, at the end of the chapter, are called:

2.title: The tribe of Judah in focus
2.text: After listing the twelve sons of Israel, the chronicler dives into the tribe of Judah — because from it will come the royal line of David and, ultimately, the Messiah.

2.question: Who bore Pharez and Zerah, sons of Judah?
This opening section details the descendants of which son of Israel?
Who was the firstborn of Jesse?
Caleb the son of Hezron begat children of which wife?
Ram, Bunah and Oren are listed as sons of whom?
What is said of Seled, son of Nadab?
Why did Sheshan give a daughter to his Egyptian servant Jarha?
options: Because he had no sons, only daughters

2.question: Helez and Eleasah, mentioned here, belong to the line of:
Raham, Jorkoam and Shammai are part of the families of:
Caleb's daughter named at the end of this list was called:
The families of scribes dwelling at Jabez were the Kenites, descended from:
options: Hemath, father of the house of Rechab

3.title: The royal line of David
3.text: The chapter lists David's sons and the succession of Judah's kings descended from Solomon, preserving the Davidic dynasty down to after the exile — with Zerubbabel.

3.question: How long did David reign in Hebron before reigning in Jerusalem?
Elishama, Eliada and Eliphelet are listed as sons of whom?
In this line of kings, who was the father of Josiah?
Zerubbabel, key figure of the return from exile, is listed here as son of:
The seven sons of Elioenai close the royal line after which event?

4.title: The prayer of Jabez
4.text: Jabez was more honourable than his brethren and called on the God of Israel: 'Oh that thou wouldest bless me indeed, and enlarge my coast, and that thine hand might be with me.' And God granted him what he requested.

4.question: How many wives did Ashur the father of Tekoa have?
What did God do when Jabez prayed for blessing and to enlarge his coast?
options: He granted him what he requested

4.question: Iru, Elah and Naam are listed as sons of:
Amnon, Rinnah and Ben-hanan are named among the sons of:
Shallum, Mibsam and Mishma are part of the descendants of which tribe?
The cities of Beth-marcaboth and Hazar-susim belonged to the Simeonites until the reign of:
Ziza the son of Shiphi is listed among the princes of which tribe?
The Simeonites, at the end of the chapter, defeated and occupied the land of whom?

5.title: Reuben loses the birthright
5.text: Reuben was Israel's firstborn, but for defiling his father's bed the birthright passed to the sons of Joseph. The chapter traces the tribes east of the Jordan: Reuben, Gad and the half tribe of Manasseh.

5.question: Beerah, prince of the Reubenites, was carried captive by which king?
The children of Gad dwelt over against the Reubenites in which land?
In which regions did the Gadites dwell?
options: Gilead, Bashan and the suburbs of Sharon

5.question: In a war, how much livestock did the tribes east of the Jordan take from the Hagarites?
options: Fifty thousand camels and much more

5.question: Why did God stir up the kings of Assyria to carry away Reuben, Gad and half Manasseh?
options: Because of their unfaithfulness (transgression) against the Lord

6.title: The temple singers
6.text: David set the Levites over the service of song in the house of the Lord, after the ark had rest. Asaph, Heman and Ethan ministered with singing before the tabernacle until Solomon built the temple.

6.question: Abishua, Bukki and Uzzi belong to the priestly line of which son of Levi?
The priest Azariah served in the house (temple) that was built by:
Jehozadak was carried captive when the Lord took Judah and Jerusalem by the hand of:
Joah, Iddo and Zerah are part of the families of which tribe?
Amasai and Ahimoth are listed as sons of:
Shimea, Haggiah and Asaiah make up a line of which tribe?
Zuph, Elkanah and Mahath are part of the line leading to which singer?
Michael, Baaseiah and Malchiah appear in the genealogy of which Levite singer?
Mahli, Mushi and Merari, named here, are of the line of singers descended from:
Zadok and Ahimaaz continue the line of high priests descended from:
Ashan and Beth-shemesh, with their suburbs, were cities given to:
What did the children of Israel give to the Levites?
Aner and Bileam, given to the family of the sons of Kohath, came from which tribe?
options: From the half tribe of Manasseh

6.question: Kedesh in Galilee, given with its suburbs, belonged to the territory of which tribe?
Heshbon and Jazer, at the end of the chapter, are listed among the cities given to the:

7.title: The northern tribes
7.text: The chronicler records Issachar, Benjamin, Naphtali, Manasseh, Ephraim and Asher, numbering their valiant men. He thus shows that all Israel — not only Judah and Levi — belongs to the covenant people.

7.question: The eighty-seven thousand men of valour numbered here belonged to which tribe?
Jeush, Benjamin and Ehud are listed as sons of Bilhan, of the tribe of:
Who had daughters, and not sons, according to this verse?
The men of Gath slew sons of Ephraim when these came down to:
This line of Ephraim culminates in which famous successor of Moses?
Heber, who begat Japhlet, Shomer and Hotham, belonged to which tribe?
Suah, Harnepher and Shual are listed as sons of Zophah, of the tribe of:
The twenty-six thousand men of valour at the end of the chapter were chiefs of which tribe?

8.title: The line of Saul
8.text: This chapter details the tribe of Benjamin, with special attention to the family of Saul, Israel's first king, and his son Jonathan — preparing for the account of Saul's fall in chapter 10.

8.question: Gera, Shephuphan and Huram are listed as descendants of which tribe?
Jeuz, Shachia and Mirma are called, at the end of the verse:
options: Heads of the fathers (of paternal houses)

8.question: Michael, Ispah and Joha are listed as sons of:
Adaiah, Beraiah and Shimrath are sons of:
Jaresiah, Eliah and Zichri, sons of Jeroham, belonged to which tribe?
Mikloth begat Shimeah; these dwelt, with their brethren, in which city?
This line of Benjamin passing through Ahaz and Azmaveth leads to the family of which king?
The sons of Ulam, mighty men of valour and skilled archers, were descendants of:

9.title: Those who repopulated Jerusalem
9.text: Closing the genealogies, the chronicler lists those who returned from captivity and dwelt again in Jerusalem: Israelites, priests, Levites, gatekeepers and singers who served in the house of God.

9.question: Asaiah, the firstborn, listed here, was of the:
The nine hundred fifty-six men numbered here are called:
options: Heads of the fathers in the houses of their fathers

9.question: The thousand seven hundred sixty men described were able for the work of the:
options: Ministry of the house of God

9.question: Who kept watch at the king's gate, eastward?
options: The gatekeepers of the sons of Levi

9.question: What were the gatekeepers in charge of, by courses?
options: The gates of the house of the Lord (the tent)

9.question: Some Levites had charge of:
options: The vessels of ministry, counted in and out

9.question: The Levite singers were free from other service because:
options: They were busy in their work day and night

9.question: Mikloth begat Shimeam; this section resumes the genealogy of which royal family?
Azel had six sons (Azrikam, Bocheru and others); he belonged to the line of:

10.title: Why Saul died
10.text: The chronicler sums up: Saul died for his transgression against the Lord, for not keeping his word, and for consulting a medium instead of seeking God. Therefore the Lord slew him and turned the kingdom to David.

10.question: How did Saul die in the battle of Mount Gilboa?
options: He fell upon his own sword

10.question: What did the Philistines do with Saul's head?
options: They fastened it in the temple of Dagon

10.question: Why did the Lord slay Saul and turn the kingdom to David?
options: Because Saul did not seek the Lord

11.title: The conquest of Zion
11.text: The Jebusites said to David, 'Thou shalt not come hither.' But David took the stronghold of Zion, which came to be called the city of David — his new capital.

11.question: What did David take from the Jebusites, which became the city of David?
Why did David wax greater and greater?
options: Because the Lord of hosts was with him

11.question: When the mighty men defended the parcel of barley and slew the Philistines, the Lord:
options: Saved them by a great deliverance

11.question: Abishai, brother of Joab, gained a name among the three by:
options: Slaying three hundred with his spear

11.question: Benaiah was the most honourable of the thirty, but reached not the three; David set him over:
Maharai and Heled, named in the list, are called:
Ahiam the son of Sacar, listed among the mighty men, is called a:
Ira and Gareb, in the list of David's mighty men, are called:
Eliel, Obed and Jasiel close which list of the chapter?

12.title: 'Thine are we, O David!'
12.text: The Spirit came upon Amasai, chief of the captains, who declared: 'Thine are we, O David, and on thy side, thou son of Jesse: peace, peace be unto thee... for thy God helpeth thee.' And David received them as captains.

12.question: Joelah and Zebadiah, sons of Jeroham of Gedor, joined David while he was:
options: A fugitive (at Ziklag), away from Saul

12.question: Jeremiah and Machbanai, listed here, were warriors of which tribe, David's mighty men?
Who was seized by the Spirit and said 'Thine are we, O David!'?
The six thousand eight hundred men armed with shield and spear were of the tribe of:
Zadok, a young and valiant man, named among those who came to David, was of which family?
The fifty thousand of Zebulun who came to David stood out for:
options: Keeping rank with no double heart

12.question: Why did the neighbours bring bread, figs, wine and oil when David was made king?
options: Because there was joy in Israel

13.title: The death of Uzza
13.text: When the oxen stumbled at the threshingfloor of Chidon, Uzza put forth his hand to hold the ark of God — and God struck him there. The improper transport (on a cart, rather than carried by the Levites) cost him his life.

13.question: Why did all the congregation agree to bring the ark?
options: Because the thing was right in the eyes of all the people

13.question: What happened when Uzza put forth his hand to hold the ark?
options: He was struck dead by God

13.question: What happened to the house of Obed-edom while the ark stayed with him three months?
options: The Lord blessed it, and all that he had

14.title: David inquires of God
14.text: Before facing the Philistines, David asked God whether he should go up against them. God directed him, and David defeated them — then won again by following a strategy God revealed to him.

14.question: Elishama, Beeliada and Eliphelet are listed as sons of David born in:
What did David command be done with the idols the Philistines left behind?
What was the result of David's victories over the Philistines?
options: His fame went out into all lands

15.title: The Levites carry the ark
15.text: This time David did it right: 'the children of the Levites bare the ark of God upon their shoulders... as Moses commanded.' The mistake that killed Uzza was corrected, and the ark went up to Jerusalem with music and joy.

15.question: Whom did David gather to bring the ark the right way?
options: The sons of Aaron and the Levites

15.question: Amminadab and his brethren, named here, were of the sons of:
How did the Levites bear the ark of God this time?
options: Upon their shoulders, with staves, as Moses commanded

15.question: Zechariah, Aziel and Shemiramoth played which instrument in the procession?
Who blew the trumpets before the ark of God?
Who despised David in her heart when she saw him dancing before the ark?

16.title: The psalm of thanksgiving
16.text: Once the ark was set in the tent, David delivered to the singers a psalm: 'Give thanks unto the Lord, call upon his name... sing psalms unto him.' It is a hymn proclaiming that the Lord reigns and that all the gods of the nations are idols.

16.question: Why did David set Levites before the ark of the Lord?
options: To record, thank and praise the Lord

16.question: What does the psalm command to do to the Lord?
options: Sing psalms to him and talk of his wondrous works

16.question: According to the psalm, where are the judgments of the Lord our God?
The psalm recalls how the patriarchs went:
options: From nation to nation, from one kingdom to another people

16.question: What does the psalm declare about the gods of the nations?
options: They are all idols; but the Lord made the heavens

16.question: What does the psalm proclaim should be said among the nations?
What does the psalm ask God to do for his people?
options: Save them and deliver them from the nations

16.question: Where was the tabernacle of the Lord that Zadok and the priests served?
options: In the high place that was at Gibeon

16.question: What did David do at the end of the celebration of the ark?
options: He returned to bless his house

17.title: The covenant with David
17.text: David wanted to build a house for God, but through Nathan the Lord answered the reverse: 'I will raise up thy seed after thee... and I will establish his kingdom.' Instead of David making God a house, God would make David a house (a dynasty) — for ever.

17.question: What does God say through Nathan about where he dwelt since bringing Israel up?
options: He dwelt in no house, but went from tent to tent

17.question: What did God promise to do when David's days were expired?
options: Raise up his seed and establish his kingdom

17.question: How did David react to the greatness of God's promise about his house?
options: He acknowledged God treated him as a man of high degree

17.question: What does David ask God in his prayer about the word given to his house?
options: That it be established for ever
That it pass to Solomon only

17.question: How does David's prayer end regarding his house?
options: That, blessed by God, it be blessed for ever

18.title: The Lord preserves David
18.text: David subdued the Philistines, Moab, Hadadezer of Zobah and the Syrians. The chronicler gives the key to it all: 'the Lord preserved David whithersoever he went.' The victories come from God, not the king's strength.

18.question: When the Syrians of Damascus came to help Hadadezer, how many did David slay?
Why did Tou send his son Hadoram to greet and bless David?
options: Because David had defeated Hadadezer, Tou's enemy

18.question: Under David's reign, Benaiah the son of Jehoiada was in charge of:

19.title: Hanun's insult
19.text: David sent messengers to comfort Hanun, king of Ammon, over his father's death. But Hanun humiliated them: he shaved them and cut off their garments in the middle. The outrage triggered the war between Ammon and Israel.

19.question: What did Hanun do to David's messengers?
options: He shaved them and cut their garments in the middle

19.question: How did the Ammonites array themselves for battle?
options: At the city gate, with the allied kings apart in the field

19.question: What happened when Joab advanced against the Syrians?
What did the servants of Hadadezer do after being defeated?
options: They made peace with David and served him

20.title: The giants of Gath
20.text: In wars with the Philistines, David's servants slew several giants — descendants of the giant of Gath. The chapter shows that, with God, not even giants resist Israel.

20.question: Whom did Sibbechai the Hushathite slay in the war at Gezer?
options: Sippai, of the children of the giant

20.question: Where were the giants who fell by the hand of David and his servants from?

21.title: The threshingfloor of Ornan
21.text: Through David's proud census came the plague; the destroying angel stopped by the threshingfloor of Ornan the Jebusite. David bought that place and built an altar there — the ground that would become the site of the temple.

21.question: How many men that drew sword were there in all Israel, according to the census?
options: A thousand thousand and a hundred thousand

21.question: What did the Lord offer David through the prophet, because of the census?
options: Three things, that he might choose one

21.question: By what place did the angel of the Lord stop when God repented of the evil?
options: The threshingfloor of Ornan the Jebusite

21.question: What was Ornan doing when he saw the angel?
How much did David pay Ornan for the place?
options: Six hundred shekels of gold by weight

21.question: Why could David not go to Gibeon to inquire of God before the tabernacle?
options: He was afraid because of the sword of the angel

22.title: Solomon, a man of rest
22.text: God said David's son would be 'a man of rest', to whom he would give peace from all enemies: so his name would be Solomon (from 'shalom', peace). It would be he, and not David (a man of war), who would build the temple.

22.question: Why did David prepare materials in abundance for the temple before he died?
options: Because Solomon was young and the house must be magnifical

22.question: Why would David's son be called Solomon?
options: Because he would be a man of rest, and God would give Israel peace
In honour of a foreign king
Because he was born in war

22.question: What did David say he had prepared in his trouble for the house of the Lord?
options: A hundred thousand talents of gold and a million of silver, and brass and iron

22.question: What did David command the leaders to do with heart and soul?
options: Seek the Lord and build the sanctuary

23.title: The courses of the Levites
23.text: Old and full of days, David made Solomon king and organized the Levites into courses according to the three sons of Levi — Gershon, Kohath and Merari — for the service of the house of the Lord.

23.question: Into how many lines (sons of Levi) did David divide the Levites by courses?
Why were Jeush and Beriah counted as one family?
options: Because they had not many sons

23.question: Rehabiah, chief of the sons of Eliezer, had a posterity that:
How did the sons of Kish take the daughters of Eleazar to wife?
options: Because Eleazar died with no sons, only daughters

23.question: Why would the Levites no longer need to carry the tabernacle?
options: Because the ark would have a fixed place (in Jerusalem)

23.question: What was the Levites' charge, according to the end of the chapter?
options: The service of the tent and sanctuary, helping the sons of Aaron

24.title: The twenty-four priestly courses
24.text: David divided the priests, sons of Aaron, into twenty-four courses, casting lots among the descendants of Eleazar and of Ithamar — impartially, since both houses gave chiefs to the sanctuary.

24.question: Why did the sons of Eleazar have more chiefs than those of Ithamar?
options: Because they were far more numerous

24.question: Malchijah and Mijamin, named here, received by lot a place among:
Bilgah and Immer are listed among the twenty-four:
Delaiah and Maaziah complete the list of the twenty-four courses of:
Jeriah, Amariah and Jahaziel are listed among the sons of:
Beno, Shoham and Zaccur, named here, were sons of the Levites descended from:
How were the remaining Levites assigned, equally with the sons of Aaron?

25.title: Music as a prophetic ministry
25.text: David set apart the sons of Asaph, Heman and Jeduthun to 'prophesy' with harps, psalteries and cymbals. The temple music was treated as a sacred ministry, with twenty-four courses assigned by lot.

25.question: What had God given to Heman, the king's seer?
The course of Zaccur, with his sons and brethren, numbered how many?
Jeshaiah, with his sons and brethren, formed a course of singers of:
Shubael, with his sons and brethren, received by lot a course of:
Hanani, named here, was head of one of the courses of:
Romamti-ezer closes which list, with the twenty-fourth course?

26.title: Gatekeepers and treasurers
26.text: The chapter organizes the courses of the gatekeepers — assigned by lot for each side of the temple — and the Levites set over the treasures of the dedicated things and over the king's affairs.

26.question: Why did Peulthai and the other sons of Obed-edom prosper so much?
Hilkiah, Tebaliah and Zechariah are listed among the:
The courses of gatekeepers were assigned to which part of the temple, per this verse?
options: Each side (e.g. the west, the gate Shallecheth)

26.question: Jehieli, named here, was head of the fathers among the sons of:
What were Shelomith and his brethren in charge of?
options: The treasures of the dedicated things consecrated by David and the chiefs

26.question: Over whom did king David set the two thousand seven hundred men of valour?
options: The Reubenites, Gadites and half tribe of Manasseh

27.title: The organization of the kingdom
27.text: David organized the army into twelve divisions serving by courses, a month each; he named chiefs over the tribes, officers over his goods and counselors. Notably, he did not number those under twenty, recalling God's promise to multiply Israel as the stars.

27.question: Benaiah the son of Jehoiada was captain of the division of which month?
Each monthly division of David's army had how many men?
Benaiah the Pirathonite commanded the army division of the:
Ishmaiah and Jerimoth, named here, were rulers over the:
Why did David not take the number of those under twenty years?
options: Because the Lord had promised to multiply Israel as the stars

27.question: Baal-hanan the Gederite and Joash were officers over:
Who was chief of the king's army, according to the end of the chapter?

28.title: 'Be strong, and do it'
28.text: Before all the leaders of Israel, David declared that the Lord had chosen Solomon to build the temple, handed him the 'pattern' (detailed model) of the house, and exhorted him: 'be strong, and do it'.

28.question: According to what God told David, who would build the house and courts of the Lord?
options: Solomon, chosen by God as a son

28.question: How did David exhort Solomon regarding the work of the sanctuary?
What did David hand Solomon with detailed weights of gold and silver?
options: The pattern of the vessels, such as the candlesticks

28.question: Who did David say would be with Solomon for all the work of the house of God?
options: The priests, Levites, willing skilful men and all the people

29.title: 'Thine, O Lord, is the greatness'
29.text: Before all the assembly, David praised: 'Thine, O Lord, is the greatness, and the power, and the glory.' The king and the people acknowledged that all they offered for the temple came from God's own hand.

29.question: Where did the fine gold David gave to overlay the walls of the house come from?
Why did the people rejoice as they gave for the temple?
options: Because they offered willingly, with perfect heart, to the Lord

29.question: How did David describe the people's condition before God in his prayer?
options: Strangers and sojourners, whose days are as a shadow

29.question: What did David ask God for his son Solomon?
options: A perfect heart to keep the commandments and build the temple

29.question: What did the princes and David's sons do concerning Solomon?
options: They submitted (swore obedience) to king Solomon

29.question: The end of the chapter sums up David's acts, with all his:
options: Reign and might, and the times that went over him
